svg.text: UR Subnet — Mechanism at a glance
How money moves in three coupled channels (all denominated in the subnet's α token). §1 of the whitepaper.
holds NO α only — never holds
carry ingress / egress traffic. Inside a NO's pool — NOT on-chain UIDs
(subnet cap ≈ 256), so each is a client_id paid by Merkle claim.
claim α directly from the contract with an O(log N) proof.
standard 18 / 41 / 41 split — we do not fight the coinbase
anyone who stakes own α and
runs /verify trails. No NO, no pool.
— Subtensor EVM · custodian + deposit ledger + 7-day settlement
Dn = SUM(DT) per NO, per epoch.
emission ever touches a NO's keys.
NO commits a payout root each epoch;
it directs the split but never holds α.
paid out by each validator's verified,
VERIFIER.md /verify trails: walk provider chains, measuring liveness & quality Q_n
deposit = objective demand anchor × quality (Q_n) = validator-measured modulator — Yuma turns it into miner emission
at bootstrap, governance caps the quality swing and widens it as the validator set and data mature
